scope.context_note: Volume III section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Sequences', section 'Order-Theoretic Limit Constructions'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.

review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: Decreasing Sequence Limit as Infimum
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a decreasing real sequence that is bounded below. Then
\[
  \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be a decreasing real sequence that is bounded below. Then
\[
  \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Decreasing Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{decreasing} if \[ x_{n+1} \le x_n \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Infimum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

direct_dependencies.title: Sequence Bounded Below
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded below} exactly when there exists $m \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $m \le x_n$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. A sequence that converge...

transitive_dependencies.title: Decreasing Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{decreasing} if \[ x_{n+1} \le x_n \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Increasing Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{increasing} if \[ x_n \le x_{n+1} \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Infimum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Lower Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, $A\subseteq S$, and $l\in S$. The element $l$ is a \emph{lower bound} of $A$ if and only if $l$ is a bound for $A$ from below, meaning every $x\in A$ satisfies $l\le x$.

transitive_dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence Bounded Above
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded above} exactly when there exists $M \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $x_n \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence Bounded Below
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded below} exactly when there exists $m \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $m \le x_n$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Supremum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

ancestor_tree.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

dependencies.title: Infimum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Variable

dependencies.title: Bound

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound

nodes.title: Increasing Sequence Limit as Supremum
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be an increasing real sequence that is bounded above. Then
\[
  \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be an increasing real sequence that is bounded above. Then
\[
  \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}.
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Increasing Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{increasing} if \[ x_n \le x_{n+1} \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Sequence Bounded Above
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded above} exactly when there exists $M \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $x_n \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

direct_dependencies.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. ancestor_tree.title: Sequence Bounded Above

dependencies.title: Upper Bound

ancestor_tree.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

dependencies.title: Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Decreasing Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{decreasing} if \[ x_{n+1} \le x_n \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Infimum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Sequence Bounded Below
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded below} exactly when there exists $m \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $m \le x_n$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

nodes.title: Tail Suprema and Infima Converge
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. Define
\[
  s_n=\sup\{x_k:k\ge n\},
  \qquad
  i_n=\inf\{x_k:k\ge n\}.
\]
Then $(s_n)$ and $(i_n)$ converge.
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. Define
\[
  s_n=\sup\{x_k:k\ge n\},
  \qquad
  i_n=\inf\{x_k:k\ge n\}.
\]
Then $(s_n)$ and $(i_n)$ converge.

direct_dependencies.title: Tail Infimum Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. The \textbf{tail infimum sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(i_n)$ defined by \[ i_n:=\inf\{x_k:k\ge n\} \qquad\text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Tail Supremum Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. The \textbf{tail supremum sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(s_n)$ defined by \[ s_n:=\sup\{x_k:k\ge n\} \qquad\text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

direct_dependencies.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

direct_dependencies.title: Tail Infima Are Increasing
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence, and let $(i_n)$ be its tail infimum sequence. Then $(i_n)$ is increasing.

direct_dependencies.title: Tail Suprema Are Decreasing
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence, and let $(s_n)$ be its tail supremum sequence. Then $(s_n)$ is decreasing.

transitive_dependencies.title: Bounded Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded} exactly when there exists $M > 0$ such that $|x_n| \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. transitive_dependencies.title: Decreasing Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{decreasing} if \[ x_{n+1} \le x_n \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Increasing Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{increasing} if \[ x_n \le x_{n+1} \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Lower Bound

transitive_dependencies.title: $M$-Tail of a Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $M \in \mathbb{N}$. The \textbf{$M$-tail} of $(x_n)$ is the sequence \[ (x_n)_{n \ge M} \;:=\; \bigl(x_M,\, x_{M+1},\, x_{M+2},\, \ldots\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Supremum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Tail Infimum Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. The \textbf{tail infimum sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(i_n)$ defined by \[ i_n:=\inf\{x_k:k\ge n\} \qquad\text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Tail Supremum Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. The \textbf{tail supremum sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(s_n)$ defined by \[ s_n:=\sup\{x_k:k\ge n\} \qquad\text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset Inclusion Preserves Upper Bounds
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq B\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. If $u$ is an upper bound of $B$, then $u$ is an upper bound of $A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

transitive_dependencies.title: Supremum Is Monotone Under Inclusion
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A,B\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty sets that are bounded above. If $A\subseteq B$, then $\sup A\le \sup B$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Tail Infima Are Increasing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence, and let $(i_n)$ be its tail infimum sequence. Then $(i_n)$ is increasing.

transitive_dependencies.title: Tail Suprema Are Decreasing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence, and let $(s_n)$ be its tail supremum sequence. Then $(s_n)$ is decreasing.

ancestor_tree.title: Tail Infimum Sequence
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. The \textbf{tail infimum sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(i_n)$ defined by \[ i_n:=\inf\{x_k:k\ge n\} \qquad\text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

dependencies.title: Bounded Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded} exactly when there exists $M > 0$ such that $|x_n| \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

dependencies.title: Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Infimum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. ancestor_tree.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence

A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

dependencies.title: Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Infimum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Language
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set

ancestor_tree.title: Tail Infima Are Increasing
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence, and let $(i_n)$ be its tail infimum sequence. Then $(i_n)$ is increasing.

dependencies.title: Infimum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property

dependencies.title: Tail Infimum Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. The \textbf{tail infimum sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(i_n)$ defined by \[ i_n:=\inf\{x_k:k\ge n\} \qquad\text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

dependencies.title: Bounded Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded} exactly when there exists $M > 0$ such that $|x_n| \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

dependencies.title: Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, $A\subseteq S$, and $l\in S$. The element $l$ is a \emph{lower bound} of $A$ if and only if $l$ is a bound for $A$ from below, meaning every $x\in A$ satisfies $l\le x$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: $M$-Tail of a Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $M \in \mathbb{N}$. The \textbf{$M$-tail} of $(x_n)$ is the sequence \[ (x_n)_{n \ge M} \;:=\; \bigl(x_M,\, x_{M+1},\, x_{M+2},\, \ldots\bigr). \]

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

dependencies.title: Peano System

ancestor_tree.title: Tail Suprema Are Decreasing
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence, and let $(s_n)$ be its tail supremum sequence. Then $(s_n)$ is decreasing.

dependencies.title: Tail Supremum Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a bounded real sequence. The \textbf{tail supremum sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(s_n)$ defined by \[ s_n:=\sup\{x_k:k\ge n\} \qquad\text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

dependencies.title: Bounded Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded} exactly when there exists $M > 0$ such that $|x_n| \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Variable
